hero.subCopy: AI-powered threat intelligence that helps you recognize phishing, deepfakes, QR scams, and social engineering — before they reach you. Real awareness. Real protection.
hero.title: Protect Yourself From Cyber Threats
hero.subtitle: Learn cyber safety, scam awareness, and digital protection.

scam-analyzer.title: AI Scam Analyzer
scam-analyzer.subtitle: Neural Threat Recognition
scam-analyzer.description: Feed suspicious messages, calls, or links into our AI engine. Detects social engineering patterns, urgency manipulation, and impersonation tactics in real time.

url-scanner.title: URL Safety Scanner
url-scanner.subtitle: Link Threat Assessment
url-scanner.description: Paste any suspicious link for instant deep analysis. Detects typosquatting, malware payloads, phishing pages, fake login portals, and redirect-based deception chains.

deepfake-lab.title: Deepfake Detection Lab
deepfake-lab.subtitle: Synthetic Media Analysis
deepfake-lab.description: Upload images or video clips. Our multimodal AI identifies facial synthesis artifacts, voice cloning signatures, and AI-generated content used in fraud and disinformation.

qr-scanner.title: QR Scam Scanner
qr-scanner.subtitle: Malicious Code Detection
qr-scanner.description: Scan any QR code before you pay or visit. Our engine decodes hidden redirect chains, UPI manipulation, malware distribution URLs, and payment fraud embedded in QR.

phishing-sim.title: Phishing Simulator
phishing-sim.subtitle: Awareness Training Engine
phishing-sim.description: Experience simulated phishing attacks in a safe environment. Learn to identify deceptive links, spoofed domains, fake login pages, and urgency-based manipulation tactics.

cyber-laws.title: Legal AI Command Center
cyber-laws.subtitle: Complaint Intelligence System
cyber-laws.description: Transform cybercrime incident narratives into structured complaints with guided interviews, legal mapping, evidence summaries, and PDF export.

challenges.title: Awareness Challenges
challenges.subtitle: Cyber Defense Training
challenges.description: Gamified cyber awareness missions. Identify scam screenshots, decode phishing emails, spot fake websites — build real-world recognition skills through immersive challenges.

reporting.title: Reporting Center
reporting.subtitle: Incident Documentation
reporting.description: Structured cyber incident reporting with AI-assisted form completion. Automatically routes reports to CERT-In, Cyber Crime Portal, or RBI Ombudsman based on incident type.

upi-fraud.title: UPI Fraud Awareness
upi-fraud.subtitle: Payment Safety Intelligence
upi-fraud.description: Understand how UPI scams operate — collect requests, screen share fraud, fake payment screenshots, and merchant impersonation. Interactive fraud map of common attack patterns.

ip-scanner.title: IP Intelligence Scanner
ip-scanner.subtitle: Network Origin Analysis
ip-scanner.description: Analyze IP addresses for threat reputation, geolocation, hosting provider, and blacklist status. Understand if a caller, website, or sender originates from known threat infrastructure.

threat-feed.title: Live Threat Feed
threat-feed.subtitle: Global Cyber Intelligence
threat-feed.description: Real-time aggregation of emerging scam campaigns, ransomware spreads, and social fraud vectors from public threat intelligence sources across India and globally.

scam-library.title: Scam Intelligence Archive
scam-library.subtitle: Fraud Pattern Database
scam-library.description: Comprehensive classified archive of documented scams, fraud schemes, and deception patterns. Search historical scam campaigns, analyze fraud tactics, and explore indexed threat intelligence from India's cyber threat landscape.

coreModules.title: Everything You Need to Stay Protected

complaint.salutation: To The Station House Officer / Cyber Crime Cell,
complaint.bodyIntroTemplate: I, {{victimName}}, respectfully submit this formal complaint to report a cybercrime incident that has occurred against me.
complaint.preparedBy: Prepared by TrustLayerLabs Sentinel Legal AI using deterministic legal mappings and user-provided facts.

cyberJustice.greeting: Tell me what happened in simple words. I will help classify the cybercrime, identify useful evidence, build a timeline, and prepare a complaint draft.
cyberJustice.greetingShort: Tell me what happened in simple words.
cyberJustice.captured: I captured those details. Review the proposed updates, then confirm them when ready.
cyberJustice.analysisResult: I classified this as {{category}} with {{confidence}}% confidence and extracted available identifiers.
cyberJustice.title: Cyber Justice AI

questions.upi-utr: What is the UTR, RRN, or transaction reference number for the payment?
questions.upi-amount: How much money was lost in this incident?
questions.phishing-url: What suspicious link or website did you receive or open?
questions.phishing-email: Do you have the sender email address or reply-to email?
questions.sextortion-platform: Which platform did the threats, messages, or impersonation happen on?
questions.suspect-phone: What phone number did the suspect use, if any?
questions.bank-name: Which bank account or bank app was used for the transaction?
questions.evidence-refs: What evidence do you have, such as screenshots, chats, emails, recordings, or bank statements?
questions.general-narrative: Please describe what happened in your own words, including contact method, money loss, and any evidence you have.

conversation.title: Conversation intake
conversation.proposedUpdates: Proposed case updates are ready for review.
conversation.placeholder: Add details naturally: I lost INR 25000, UTR was..., the suspect used WhatsApp...

header.title: Build a legal-ready cybercrime case file.
header.subtitle: Narrative analysis, evidence vault, timeline builder, party details, financial loss, complaint generation, and PDF export now share one case file.

emergency.title: Emergency financial fraud?
emergency.desc: Call 1930 immediately and contact your bank to try freezing funds.

legalOutput.title: Legal engine output
legalOutput.noAnalysis: The classification and laws will appear here after analysis.

timeline.title: Timeline builder
timeline.description: Generate events from the conversation, extracted entities, and evidence, then add manual corrections.

name.label: Name
name.placeholder: WhatsApp screenshot, bank statement, call recording...

extractable.placeholder: Paste OCR text, email body, chat export, transcript, statement text, UTR/RRN, UPI IDs, URLs, wallet IDs...

analysis.noNarrative: Add an incident narrative first, then run analysis.
analysis.title: Classification and laws
analysis.description: Review the mapped Sentinel Legal analysis before collecting evidence.

review.title: Review center
review.description: Validate the complete Phase 2 case file before generating the complaint and PDF.
review.officialPacketDesc: Creates a complaint packet with cover page, annexures, page numbers, and signature block.

submission.noPacket: No official complaint packet generated yet. Use "Generate Packet" to create an export-ready complaint packet.

checklist.title: Submission checklist
checklist.allSet: All set — no immediate checklist items.

instagram.title: Instagram contact established
instagram.description: The narrative mentions the suspect first contacted the victim through Instagram.
instagram.reason: Social media platform was explicitly referenced in the incident report.

videoCall.title: Video call interaction occurred
videoCall.description: A video call happened during the incident.
videoCall.reason: The user described a video call exchange, which is a key timeline event in sextortion cases.

recording.title: Recording or footage was captured
recording.description: A recording or similar media was referenced in the incident narrative.
recording.reason: Recording of the victim or interaction can anchor an extortion timeline event.

threat.title: Threat or extortion demand was issued
threat.description: A direct threat or extortion demand was described by the victim.
threat.reason: Threat language is an important event marker for sextortion timelines.

paymentDemand.title: Payment demand was requested
paymentDemand.description: The user mentioned a monetary demand or amount in the narrative.
paymentDemand.reason: Monetary demands are essential to reconstruct the incident timeline.

narrative.title: Incident described by complainant
narrative.reason: The user provided a natural-language incident account.

evidence.title: {{type}} evidence available
evidence.reason: Evidence vault item has a timestamp and can anchor the case timeline.

entityGroups.reason: The deterministic entity extractor found identifiers that may belong on the incident timeline.

preserveEvidence.title: Preserve evidence
preserveEvidence.detail: Take screenshots, save messages, download attachments and do not delete logs.

contactHelpline.title: Call 1930
contactHelpline.detail: Contact National Cyber Crime Helpline 1930 for guidance and immediate assistance.

call1930.title: Call 1930
call1930.detail: Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline 1930 immediately and report the unauthorized transaction.

contactBank.title: Contact your bank
contactBank.detail: Notify your bank, request account freeze and reversal, provide transaction IDs and evidence.

otpCall.title: Call 1930
otpCall.detail: Report OTP interception or misuse via the helpline for immediate advice.

notifyBank.title: Notify bank
notifyBank.detail: Inform the bank and request account freeze. Change banking credentials and enable stronger 2FA.

preservePhishing.title: Preserve phishing sample
preservePhishing.detail: Save the email, screenshot the URL and download headers where possible.

doNotClick.title: Do not click links
doNotClick.detail: Do not follow any links or provide credentials. Change passwords for exposed accounts.

reportPlatform.title: Report to platform
reportPlatform.detail: Report the phishing page to the hosting platform and to CERT-In / National portal.

doNotPay.title: Do not pay
doNotPay.detail: Do not pay or negotiate with extortionists — payment often escalates demands.

contactPolice.title: Contact Cyber Police
contactPolice.detail: File a complaint with local cyber cell and the National Cyber Crime Portal.

blockReport.title: Block and report
blockReport.detail: Block the perpetrator on platforms and report to platform grievance officers.

freezeAccounts.title: Freeze affected accounts
freezeAccounts.detail: Contact banks and platforms to freeze or secure accounts.

collectProof.title: Collect proof
collectProof.detail: Gather evidence of impersonation, fake profiles, and transactions.

changeCredentials.title: Change credentials
changeCredentials.detail: Change passwords for affected accounts and enable MFA immediately.

notifyPlatforms.title: Notify affected platforms
notifyPlatforms.detail: Inform service providers and follow their incident response guidance.

reportCert.title: Report to CERT-In
reportCert.detail: File a report with CERT-In for technical advisory and mitigation.

isolateSystems.title: Isolate affected systems
isolateSystems.detail: Disconnect compromised devices from network to prevent further access.

preserveLogs.title: Preserve logs
preserveLogs.detail: Collect and preserve logs, system images and timestamps for forensic analysis.

upi-transaction-screenshot.title: Transaction screenshot
upi-transaction-screenshot.description: Upload a screenshot showing the fraudulent UPI transaction, amount, UPI ID, or reference number.
upi-transaction-screenshot.reason: This proves the transfer details and supports bank escalation.

upi-bank-statement.title: Bank statement
upi-bank-statement.description: Upload a statement or passbook entry showing the debit.
upi-bank-statement.reason: This validates the claimed financial loss.

phishing-page-record.title: Phishing page or message record
phishing-page-record.description: Upload the suspicious page, email, or message that led to the incident.
phishing-page-record.reason: This preserves the deception path and malicious URL before it disappears.

sextortion-threat-record.title: Threat messages or recordings
sextortion-threat-record.description: Upload chats, screenshots, audio, or video showing threats or coercion.
sextortion-threat-record.reason: Threat content is central to intimidation and extortion claims.

profile-impersonation-proof.title: Profile or impersonation proof
profile-impersonation-proof.description: Upload screenshots of fake profiles, job posts, or impersonation pages.
profile-impersonation-proof.reason: This connects the accused identity or fake profile to the harm.

crypto-wallet-transaction.title: Wallet or investment transaction record
crypto-wallet-transaction.description: Upload wallet transfer proof, investment dashboard screenshots, or payment records.
crypto-wallet-transaction.reason: This links wallet IDs and payment trail to the complaint.

communication-history.title: Communication history
communication-history.description: Upload chats, emails, or screenshots showing how the suspect contacted you.
communication-history.reason: Communication records explain the deception and suspect identifiers.

nationalPortal.name: National Cyber Crime Portal
nationalPortal.notes: Official government portal for cybercrime complaints

cyberPolice.name: Cyber Police (Local Cyber Cell)
cyberPolice.notes: Visit or contact your nearest cyber crime police station with printed evidence

cert.name: CERT-In
cert.notes: Computer Emergency Response Team — report breaches and incidents

phishing.notes: Report phishing incidents and provide evidence
phishing.localNotes: File FIR at local cyber cell if financial loss occurred
phishing.certNotes: CERT-In accepts technical reports of phishing campaigns

dataBreach.certNotes: Report data breaches for mitigation and advisories
dataBreach.localNotes: Notify local cyber cell for investigation and FIR

hacking.certNotes: Report intrusions and request incident response guidance
hacking.localNotes: Contact local cyber cell for criminal investigation

financial.label: Transaction and bank proof
financial.description: Transaction screenshot, bank statement, UTR/RRN, or payment reference.

communication.label: Suspect communication
communication.description: Chats, emails, screenshots, calls, or recordings showing deception, threats, or demands.

identity.label: Impersonation or account proof
identity.description: Fake profile, forged document, account compromise, or impersonation screenshots.

platform.label: Platform record
platform.description: Platform profile URL, account handle, report/takedown reference, or message export.

reasons.upi-utr: Transaction references help banks and police trace the fraudulent transfer.
reasons.upi-amount: The loss amount is needed for the complaint and financial loss annexure.
reasons.phishing-url: The URL is a primary identifier for phishing investigation.
reasons.phishing-email: Email identifiers help preserve the impersonation trail.
reasons.sextortion-platform: Platform context helps request takedown, account tracing, and evidence preservation.
reasons.suspect-phone: Phone numbers are actionable identifiers for investigation.
reasons.bank-name: Bank details support the financial loss annexure and recovery workflow.
reasons.evidence-refs: Evidence references help build the complaint packet annexures.
reasons.general-narrative: The narrative is needed before classification-specific questions can be selected.

footer.brandSubtitle: AI-powered public cyber safety — free, open, and built for everyone.
footer.copyright: Stay aware · Stay protected · Threat intelligence for everyone

nav.sub: Rapid response to cybercrime, digital fraud, phishing, identity theft, and online scams. Protecting India's digital citizens with AI-powered intelligence and 24×7 emergency guidance.
nav.copNetwork: TRUSTLAYERLABS © 2025 — CYBER EMERGENCY RESPONSE COMMAND CENTER

timeline.title: Emergency Response Timeline
timeline.sub: Follow this sequence immediately after a cyber incident. Time is critical — every minute matters for preserving evidence and limiting damage.

reporting.title: Reporting Command Center
reporting.sub: Select the type of cybercrime you've experienced. Our AI guidance system will adapt its recommendations based on your incident category.
reporting.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what happened in detail — include dates, times, amounts, platforms used by the attacker...
reporting.attackerPlaceholder: Phone number, URL, email, or UPI ID used by attacker

vault.title: Evidence Vault
vault.sub: Securely submit digital evidence for forensic analysis. All uploads are encrypted with AES-256 and stored in isolated secure containers.
vault.protocolText: All evidence is encrypted with AES-256 before transmission. Files are stored in isolated forensic containers with chain-of-custody logging. Your data is never shared without your explicit consent and is only accessible to authorized cybercrime investigators.

actions.title: Emergency Action Center
actions.sub: Execute these critical actions immediately after a cyber incident. Speed is your greatest advantage — act within the first 4 hours.

alerts.title: Live Cyber Threat Alerts
alerts.sub: Active threat campaigns identified by our intelligence network. Stay informed and share these warnings with your community.

support.title: National Cyber Support Hub
support.sub: Official channels, emergency contacts, and legal resources for cybercrime victims in India.

awareness.title: Cyber Awareness Protocol
awareness.sub: Critical behavioral intelligence to protect yourself and minimize damage during a cyber incident.

map.title: India Cyber Incident Map
map.sub: Real-time visualization of cyber threat activity across India's major cities. Data sourced from national cybercrime reporting infrastructure.

cta.sub: Every second counts after a cyber incident. Our emergency response system is active 24×7 to guide you through crisis, protect your evidence, and connect you with national law enforcement.

phishing: Do NOT click the link — it may install malware
Report the URL to Google Safe Browsing
Check if credentials were entered on the fake site
Change passwords immediately for affected accounts
upi: Immediately call your bank fraud line
Report the fraudulent UPI ID to your payment app
Call 1930 within 1 hour for best recovery chances
deepfake: Record the suspicious call if possible
Verify the identity through a separate known channel
Never transfer money based on video/voice calls alone
Report to cybercrime.gov.in with call recording
Alert your contacts — attackers may impersonate you too
impersonation: Document all communications from the impersonator
File complaint under IT Act Section 66D
Request platform takedown of fraudulent profiles
Collect all evidence before reporting to police
qrscam: Check bank statement for unauthorized debits
Report the QR code location to local police
Alert the merchant whose QR was replaced
File UPI dispute with your payment app
Collect transaction proof from app history
hacking: Change passwords from a DIFFERENT device
Check if email forwarding rules were added by attacker
investment: Do NOT invest more money expecting recovery
Collect all communication and payment receipts
Alert your bank to flag any related accounts
identity: File complaint with UIDAI if Aadhaar misused
Alert credit bureaus (CIBIL) about potential fraud
Check form 26AS for unauthorized ITR filings
Report to respective platform where identity was used
File police FIR for identity theft

timelineSteps.title: Incident Detected
timelineSteps.desc: You've identified a cyber attack, fraud attempt, or suspicious activity targeting your digital accounts or finances.
timelineSteps.expand: ⚡ STAY CALM. Do not panic — panicking leads to mistakes. Note the exact time, platform, and nature of the incident. Do NOT delete any messages, calls, or transaction history yet. Take a screenshot immediately. Your next 60 seconds determine how much evidence you preserve.

timelineSteps.title: Secure Your Accounts
timelineSteps.desc: Immediately change passwords for all critical accounts — banking, email, social media, UPI apps.
timelineSteps.expand: 🔑 PRIORITY ORDER: 1) Email account (it's the master key) 2) Banking apps 3) UPI/Payment apps 4) Social media. Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) on all accounts. If you suspect SIM swap, contact your telecom operator NOW. Log out of all active sessions from other devices.

timelineSteps.title: Preserve All Evidence
timelineSteps.desc: Document everything before attempting to undo the damage. Evidence is your most powerful legal asset.
timelineSteps.expand: 📸 COLLECT: Screenshots of all suspicious messages/calls/transactions. Transaction IDs, UTR numbers, UPI IDs. Phone numbers, email addresses, website URLs used by attackers. Record approximate time and sequence of events. Save bank SMS alerts. Do NOT format your phone or computer yet.

timelineSteps.title: Contact Your Bank
timelineSteps.desc: For financial fraud, contact your bank's 24×7 fraud helpline within the first 4 hours to maximize recovery chances.
timelineSteps.expand: 📞 CALL: Your bank's 24×7 fraud helpline immediately. Request a HOLD on the fraudulent transaction. File a formal Fraud Complaint in writing. Ask for the complaint reference number. The RBI directive requires banks to reverse unauthorized transactions reported within 3 days of notification.

timelineSteps.title: Submit Cyber Complaint
timelineSteps.desc: File an official complaint on the National Cybercrime Reporting Portal or call the helpline 1930.
timelineSteps.expand: 🌐 FILE AT: cybercrime.gov.in — available 24×7. Call: 1930 (National Cybercrime Helpline). You'll receive a complaint acknowledgement number — save it. For financial fraud over ₹1 lakh, also file at your nearest police station. Attach all evidence you've collected.

timelineSteps.title: Follow Investigation
timelineSteps.desc: Track your complaint, cooperate with investigators, and follow up regularly on the portal.
timelineSteps.expand: 📊 TRACK: Visit cybercrime.gov.in/complaint/trackComplaint to track status. Keep all communication with bank and police in writing. Follow up every 7 days if no update. Engage a cybercrime lawyer if the amount exceeds ₹5 lakh. Document all follow-up interactions with reference numbers.

emergencyCards.title: Freeze Bank Account
emergencyCards.desc: Immediately block all transactions on compromised accounts to prevent further unauthorized withdrawals.

emergencyCards.title: Contact Bank Fraud Cell
emergencyCards.desc: Report fraud to your bank's 24×7 emergency fraud helpline. Golden window: first 4 hours for transaction reversal.

emergencyCards.title: Block SIM Card
emergencyCards.desc: If SIM swap is suspected, call your telecom operator immediately. BSNL: 1503, Airtel: 121, Jio: 199, Vi: 9889.

emergencyCards.title: Report UPI Fraud
emergencyCards.desc: File UPI fraud complaint via NPCI portal or call 1800-120-1740. Also report to your payment app's fraud team.

emergencyCards.title: Change All Passwords
emergencyCards.desc: Immediately change passwords for email, banking, and social media. Use unique strong passwords for each account.

emergencyCards.title: Enable MFA Everywhere
emergencyCards.desc: Turn on multi-factor authentication for all critical accounts. Use authenticator apps over SMS where possible.

emergencyCards.title: National Cybercrime Portal
emergencyCards.desc: File official complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or call 1930. Required for police FIR and legal proceedings.

emergencyCards.title: File Police FIR
emergencyCards.desc: Visit nearest police station with all evidence for formal FIR. Mandatory for losses above ₹25,000 or identity theft.

alertCards.title: Fake RBI/SBI KYC Phishing Campaign
alertCards.desc: Fraudsters are sending SMS messages mimicking RBI and SBI requesting urgent KYC update via malicious links. Clicking installs credential-stealing malware.

alertCards.title: AI Voice Clone Fraud Calls
alertCards.desc: Highly convincing AI-cloned voice calls impersonating relatives or senior officials demanding immediate money transfer for emergencies. Verify independently.

alertCards.title: QR Code Payment Swap Attack
alertCards.desc: Fraudsters physically replacing QR codes at shops and restaurants with their own. Customers scanning think they're paying the merchant but are paying attackers.

alertCards.title: Fake Crypto/Stock Investment Platforms
alertCards.desc: Fraudulent investment apps showing fake returns encouraging victims to invest more before exit scamming. Uses fake celebrity endorsements and doctored SEBI approvals.

alertCards.title: "Digital Arrest" Extortion Scam
alertCards.desc: Callers posing as CBI/ED/Customs officers via video call claim you're under "digital arrest" for money laundering. No such thing exists in Indian law — it's fraud.

alertCards.title: Coordinated SIM Swap Banking Attacks
alertCards.desc: Fraudsters using insider telecom contacts to perform unauthorized SIM swaps, then emptying bank accounts via OTP interception before victims notice loss of signal.

tickerItems.text: Fake SBI KYC update links circulating via WhatsApp
Fake payment QR codes replacing merchant codes in Delhi NCR
AI-generated voice calls impersonating senior officials reported
Fake crypto trading platforms with celebrity endorsements spreading
Coordinated SIM swap attacks targeting Mumbai banking customers
Fake ED/CBI calls demanding payment to avoid "digital arrest"

supportCards.title: Cybercrime Helpline
supportCards.info: National cybercrime helpline operated by the Ministry of Home Affairs. Report financial cybercrime, phishing, and online fraud. Available round the clock.

supportCards.title: National Cyber Crime Portal
supportCards.info: Official government portal for filing cybercrime complaints online. Mandatory for FIR registration and tracking investigation status. Available 24×7.

supportCards.title: Digital Arrest Awareness
supportCards.info: "Digital Arrest" does not exist in Indian law. No government agency ever demands payment over video call. Hang up immediately and call 1930 to report.
supportCards.warn: NO SUCH THING AS DIGITAL ARREST

supportCards.title: Legal Rights Guidance
supportCards.info: Under IT Act Section 66C (identity theft) and 66D (cheating by impersonation), cybercriminals face up to 3 years imprisonment and ₹1 lakh fine. You have legal protection.

supportCards.title: CERT-In Emergency
supportCards.info: Indian Computer Emergency Response Team — handles critical cyber incidents. For major security breaches affecting organizations or critical infrastructure.

awarenessPanels.title: What To Do Immediately
items: Stay calm and think clearly before taking any action
Screenshot everything — messages, transaction details, URLs
Disconnect the compromised device from the internet
Call your bank's fraud helpline within 60 minutes
File complaint at cybercrime.gov.in with all evidence
Inform family and trusted contacts about the incident

awarenessPanels.title: What NOT To Do
items: Do NOT transfer money even if told it will be recovered
Do NOT share OTPs with anyone — ever — including 'bank officials'
Do NOT install apps suggested by unknown callers
Do NOT delete messages, call logs, or transaction records
Do NOT format your phone or computer before evidence collection
Do NOT engage further with the attacker — block and report

awarenessPanels.title: Emotional Manipulation Warning
items: Urgency pressure: 'Act now or lose everything' is a manipulation tactic
Fear induction: Fake arrest threats, legal action, account closure
Authority impersonation: Fake RBI/CBI/ED/police officer claims
Greed exploitation: 'You've won a prize' or 'guaranteed returns'
Isolation tactics: 'Don't tell anyone or you'll be arrested'
Love/relationship scams targeting loneliness or grief

awarenessPanels.title: Preserving Digital Evidence
items: Export full chat history — not just selected screenshots
Save bank SMS alerts and email confirmations
Note call logs with timestamps and call duration
Record full URLs — not just domain names
Back up all evidence to a secure cloud or separate device
Write a chronological account of events with exact times

bns.title: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita Explorer

cheat.title: Cheating family
cheat.desc: Online deception, fake platforms, payment traps.

forgery.title: Forgery family
forgery.desc: Fake records, fabricated proof, identity documents.

harass.title: Harassment and stalking contexts
harass.desc: Sexual harassment, stalking, image abuse contexts.

cheat.title: Cheating through digital channels
cheat.desc: Use for dishonest inducement in online investments, job scams, marketplace fraud, support impersonation, and payment manipulation.

forgery.title: Forgery and false records
forgery.desc: Relevant when screenshots, IDs, signatures, invoices, certificates, or electronic records are fabricated or used as genuine.

threat.title: Criminal intimidation
threat.desc: Covers threats to reputation, body, property, family, or livelihood delivered through chats, calls, posts, and emails.

harass.title: Harassment and stalking contexts
harass.desc: Useful for repeated unwanted contact, sexualized threats, and stalking behavior alongside IT Act provisions.

ipc.title: Indian Penal Code Cyber Links
ipc.lead: Use this searchable explorer for older references, FIR language, and cyber-adjacent offences such as cheating, forgery, defamation, intimidation, and obscenity.

ipc420.title: IPC 420
ipc420.desc: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property.

ipc471.title: IPC 471
ipc471.desc: Using forged electronic or physical records as genuine.

ipc420.title: Cheating online
ipc420.desc: Commonly referenced for cyber fraud, marketplace scams, fake investment portals, and social engineering that causes wrongful loss.

ipc468.title: Forgery for cheating
ipc468.desc: Applies when forged documents, identities, screenshots, emails, or records are created to deceive a victim or organization.

ipc471.title: Using forged records
ipc471.desc: Useful when a forged record is presented as genuine, including fake KYC, payment proof, or doctored communication.

ipc499.title: Defamation
ipc499.desc: May arise in reputational attacks, false posts, impersonation campaigns, or targeted harmful publication.

ipc503.title: Threats and intimidation
ipc503.desc: Relevant to extortion threats, doxxing threats, blackmail messages, and coercive online intimidation.

itAct.title: Information Technology Act Matrix
itAct.lead: Search practical IT Act anchors for unauthorized access, identity theft, privacy breach, obscene material, protected systems, and intermediary response.

sec43.title: Unauthorized access and damage
sec43.desc: Use for unauthorized access, data extraction, malware, denial of service, account compromise, and system damage. Preserve logs, IP traces, device images, and timestamps.

sec66C.title: Identity theft
sec66C.desc: Applies to fraudulent use of passwords, digital signatures, usernames, OTPs, or other unique identification features.

sec66D.title: Cheating by personation
sec66D.desc: Relevant for fake support agents, spoofed profiles, romance scams, job scams, marketplace impersonation, and payment redirection.

sec66E.title: Privacy violation
sec66E.desc: Covers capture, publication, or transmission of private images in violation of privacy expectations.

sec67.title: Obscene and sexually explicit material
sec67.desc: Used for publication or transmission of obscene, sexually explicit, or child sexual abuse material, with serious escalation requirements.

rights.title: Know Your Digital Rights
rights.lead: A practical rights console for cybercrime victims, students, employees, creators, and everyday internet users.

privacy.title: Right to privacy
privacy.desc: You can object to unauthorized capture, publication, or misuse of private information and intimate images.

evidence.title: Right to preserve proof
evidence.desc: You can document threats and fraud without engaging with the offender or destroying the trail.

report.title: Right to report
report.desc: Victims can approach cyber police, local police, helplines, portals, banks, platforms, and institutional authorities.

safety.title: Right to protection
safety.desc: Threats, extortion, stalking, doxxing, and sexual harassment should be escalated quickly and safely.

avoid.title: What should I avoid after an incident?
avoid.desc: Avoid paying extortion, deleting evidence, forwarding abusive content widely, or publicly naming suspects before formal reporting.

takedown.title: Can I ask for takedown?
takedown.desc: Yes. Use platform reporting tools for impersonation, abuse, non-consensual content, and fraud pages while keeping proof of the report.

penalties.title: Punishment and Penalty Cards
penalties.lead: Scan common cyber offence categories, likely legal tracks, and severity signals. Exact punishment depends on facts, applicable law, and investigation.
penalties.searchPlaceholder: Search fraud, identity, privacy, obscene, malware...

identity.title: Identity theft
identity.desc: Fraudulent use of passwords, signatures, OTPs, or identity features. Preserve account logs and impersonation proof.

personation.title: Cheating by personation
personation.desc: Fake support, fake profiles, phishing, and deception through communication devices.

privacy.title: Privacy violation
privacy.desc: Capturing or sharing private images without consent. Escalate fast and request takedown.

obscene.title: Obscene content
obscene.desc: Publication or transmission of obscene or sexually explicit material can trigger serious penalties.

unauthorized.title: Unauthorized access
unauthorized.desc: Hacking, malware, account compromise, data extraction, or system damage.

threat.title: Threat and extortion
threat.desc: Blackmail, doxxing threats, intimidation, and coercive demands may involve criminal intimidation and extortion tracks.

cases.title: Choose the Legal Response Path
cases.lead: Work through realistic cyber incidents and select the safest evidence-first response.

awareness.title: Prevention Tips With Legal Readiness
awareness.lead: Good cyber hygiene is also good evidence hygiene. These tips help reduce risk and keep response options open.

otp.title: Never share OTPs
otp.desc: No bank, police, delivery agent, or support desk should ask for OTPs, PINs, or screen-share access.

logs.title: Keep the trail
logs.desc: Do not delete messages, call logs, emails, app names, or URLs after a cyber incident.

verify.title: Verify channels
verify.desc: Use official websites, verified app stores, known customer care numbers, and direct institution portals.

extortion.title: Do not negotiate alone
extortion.desc: For blackmail, intimate image threats, or doxxing, preserve evidence and involve trusted support quickly.

before.title: Before
before.desc: Use password managers, MFA, privacy settings, and trusted contacts for account recovery.

during.title: During
during.desc: Stop interaction, capture proof, isolate accounts, and notify banks or platforms immediately.

after.title: After
after.desc: File reports, monitor accounts, update evidence, and seek institutional or legal support where needed.

quiz.title: CYBER AWARENESS QUIZ

advice.initial: Analyze the scenario carefully. Look for social engineering patterns and URL anomalies.
advice.correct: Excellent situational awareness. You identified the core phishing signal pattern.
advice.wrong: Review threat vectors: urgency signals combined with URL mismatch are primary indicators.

0.text: Which of the following is a sign of a phishing email?

options.A: Personalized greeting with your full name
options.B: Sent from your company's verified domain
options.C: Urgent language plus a mismatched sender URL
options.D: Contains a calendar invite with correct details

0.explanation: Urgency combined with mismatched sender URLs is a classic phishing signal. Attackers create artificial time pressure to override rational thinking.

quiz.aiFeedbackCorrect: Excellent situational awareness. You identified the core phishing signal pattern.
quiz.aiFeedbackWrong: Review threat vectors: urgency signals combined with URL mismatch are primary indicators.
quiz.aiFeedbackDefault: Analyze the scenario carefully. Look for social engineering patterns and URL anomalies.

0.title: Phishing Detection
0.scenario: A message says your bank account will be locked in 20 minutes and asks you to verify through bit.ly/bank-safe.
0.explanation: Urgency + shortened URLs + credential requests = textbook phishing. Never click shortened links from unsolicited messages.

1.title: Password Strength Analysis
1.scenario: Which password is strongest for a new account?
1.explanation: Length + randomness + mixed character classes = entropy. 'river-Quartz-91!vault' has superior entropy making it exponentially harder to crack.

2.title: Social Engineering Defense
2.scenario: A caller claiming to be IT asks for your OTP to close an urgent ticket.
2.explanation: Legitimate IT never requests OTPs. Always verify caller identity through official channels before taking any action.

0.title: Fake Job Offer Scam
0.body: A candidate receives a premium-looking offer letter and is asked to pay a refundable equipment fee before their start date.
0.lesson: Verify recruiter identity, company domain, and interview history. Legitimate employers never ask candidates to pay fees to receive a job offer.

1.title: UPI Refund Trap
1.body: A caller sends a QR code to receive money, but scanning it opens a collect request that prompts for your UPI PIN.
1.lesson: Entering UPI PIN always authorizes a payment FROM your account. Refunds never require a PIN entry on your device.

2.title: Account Lock Phishing
2.body: An email claims your account will be permanently closed today unless you sign in through a provided shortened link immediately.
2.lesson: Urgency, shortened links, and credential requests are the highest-risk signals. Always navigate directly to the service's official website.

1.text: Which of the following is a sign of a phishing email?

options.A: Personalized greeting with your full name

options.C: Urgent language plus a mismatched sender URL
options.D: Contains a calendar invite with correct details

1.explanation: Urgency combined with mismatched sender URLs is a classic phishing signal. Attackers create artificial time pressure to override rational thinking.

awareness.title: UPI Fraud Awareness
awareness.description: Protect your UPI account by learning key scam patterns, approval traps, and safe payment rules.

scams.0: Fake collect requests disguised as refunds, prizes, or order payments.
scams.1: Screen sharing or remote support calls asking you to open UPI apps.
scams.2: Impersonation of customer care numbers or bank officials.
scams.3: Requests to enter UPI PIN to \'receive\' money or validate a refund.
scams.4: Fake QR codes and payment links sent by strangers or buyers.

safe.0: Never share your UPI PIN, OTP, or account details with anyone.
safe.1: Use your own bank/UPI app to initiate payments ΓÇö not a link from a stranger.
safe.2: Verify the recipient VPA, amount, and purpose before approving.
safe.3: Decline unexpected collect requests and confirm refunds from the sender.
safe.4: Set daily UPI limits and enable app notifications for every transaction.

reporting.0: Report fraud at cybercrime.gov.in and retain transaction receipts.
reporting.1: Call 1930 for cybercrime support and bank help lines quickly.
reporting.2: Share UPI IDs, transaction IDs, timings, and sender details with authorities.
reporting.3: NPCI regulates UPI ΓÇö report suspicious merchant behavior to your bank.

caseStudy.title: Real UPI Scam: Refund Collect Request
caseStudy.desc: A user receives a message from a buyer on a marketplace platform. The buyer claims they will send a refund and shares a UPI payment link. The user clicks and approves the request without verifying the payee or the prompt.

whatHappened.0: The buyer sent a UPI collect request claiming to refund excess payment.
whatHappened.1: The request asked the user to approve the amount through their UPI app.
whatHappened.2: The UPI approval screen required the UPI PIN, which the user entered.
whatHappened.3: Money was debited from the user\'s account instead of being received.

whyScam.0: Real refunds do not require UPI PIN approval from the receiver.
whyScam.1: The collect request was disguised as incoming money but actually authorized a payment.
whyScam.2: The sender was an unknown contact, not a verified buyer or service provider.

0.title: Always verify payee details
0.desc: Check the VPA, name, and purpose before approving any collect request.

1.title: Never enter PIN to receive money
1.desc: Entering a UPI PIN means you are authorizing money to leave your account.

2.title: Use trusted channels
2.desc: Initiate refunds or payments from official apps instead of clicking links from strangers.

3.title: Report suspicious requests
3.desc: Report fraud quickly and keep evidence like screenshots, UPI IDs, and transaction times.

demo.title: Practice UPI Scam Detection
demo.desc: Select an example to see whether the UPI request is safe or suspicious, and learn the red flags to watch for.
demo.defaultTip: Always verify the payee name, UPI ID, and transaction context. If you are unsure, cancel and confirm directly with the sender.

report.title: Report UPI Fraud Quickly
report.desc: If you suspect UPI fraud, preserve evidence and notify authorities right away. Fast action improves your chances of recovery.

step1.0: Screenshot the UPI approval screen, collect request, and chat messages.
step1.1: Save the senderΓÇÖs phone number, UPI ID, and transaction details.
step1.2: Note the date, time, bank, and amount involved in the incident.

step2.0: Call your bankΓÇÖs fraud helpline and explain the suspicious UPI transaction.
step2.1: Request a temporary block or reset of your UPI PIN if needed.
step2.2: Ask the bank to trace the fraudulent UPI ID and freeze the transaction trail.

step3.0: Go to cybercrime.gov.in and file a complaint under UPI/payment fraud.
step3.1: Include UPI transaction IDs, messages, phone numbers, and screenshots.
step3.2: Keep copies of complaint receipts and reference numbers.

home.heroTitle: Understand UPI scams, protect your PIN, and pay safely.
home.heroDesc: This module helps you spot fake collect requests, screen-share traps, and phishing payment prompts ΓÇö without using any real payment APIs.
home.mattersDesc: UPI fraud is one of the fastest-growing payment scams in India. This lab helps you identify the red flags before you approve a transaction.
home.fact1: Fake collect requests often appear like refunds, prizes, or customer support.
home.fact2: If an app asks for your UPI PIN to receive money, treat it as a high-risk scam.
home.fact3: Always verify the recipient, the amount, and the expected flow before approving payments.
home.sectionsTitle: Explore the UPI Fraud learning path
home.sectionsDesc: Go deeper into scam awareness, real case learning, interactive UPI checks, and reporting best practices.

fastFacts.0: Never share your UPI PIN or OTP with anyone.
fastFacts.1: Validate the payee VPA and amount before approving collect requests.
fastFacts.2: Use official bank apps or UPI apps, not links sent by strangers.

watch.0: Unexpected refund or prize requests that ask for payment approval.
watch.1: Screen sharing or remote-control calls that ask you to open UPI apps.
watch.2: Impersonation of banks, merchants, or government officials.

report.1: File at cybercrime.gov.in with UPI transaction details.
report.2: Keep screenshots, UPI IDs, and bank reference numbers.

cyberFacts.1: Shortened URLs are weaponized in 64% of WhatsApp scams
cyberFacts.2: Fake courier delivery scams increased 340% this quarter
cyberFacts.3: 43% of breaches started with a phishing email
cyberFacts.4: QR code phishing attacks grew 587% in 2024
cyberFacts.5: Visual similarity attacks bypass 82% of users

urlScannerPage.headerSubtitle: Advanced {{provider}} powered threat intelligence system for analyzing suspicious URLs, domain health, and redirect logic.
urlScannerPage.aiUnavailable: VirusTotal API key is missing. Showing heuristic output.
urlScannerPage.understandingThreats: Understanding how malicious links deceive you is the first line of digital defense.

phishingWork.title: How Phishing Links Work
phishingWork.desc: Phishing links disguise themselves as legitimate URLs to steal credentials. Attackers use visual similarity, subdomain tricks, and homograph attacks to deceive users.

domainHiding.title: Domain Hiding Techniques
domainHiding.desc: Cybercriminals use sophisticated methods to conceal malicious domains and make them appear legitimate. Understanding these tactics helps you identify suspicious links before clicking.
domainHiding.item1: URL shorteners hiding the real destination
domainHiding.item2: Multiple redirects to obscure the final target

httpsEnough.title: HTTPS Alone Isn't Enough
httpsEnough.desc: The presence of HTTPS and a padlock icon does NOT guarantee a site is safe. 78% of phishing sites now use SSL certificates to appear trustworthy. Always verify the domain itself.
httpsEnough.item1: Free SSL certificates are available to anyone
httpsEnough.item2: Attackers use HTTPS to build false credibility
httpsEnough.item3: Check the domain name, not just the lock icon

qrShortened.title: QR & Shortened URL Dangers
qrShortened.desc: QR codes and shortened URLs completely hide the destination, making them perfect vectors for phishing. QR phishing (quishing) attacks increased 587% in 2024.
qrShortened.item2: Commonly used in parking, delivery, and payment scams
qrShortened.item3: Always verify the source before scanning QR codes

urlScannerPage.everyLinkThreat: Every link is a potential threat vector. Stay vigilant, verify sources, and trust your instincts.
urlScannerPage.eduPlatform: Educational awareness platform • Not affiliated with security vendors
urlScannerPage.forDemoOnly: For demonstration and learning purposes only
